item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That whole SCP tirade was worth an upvote on its own. That was beautiful.

Rest of the story is good too, of course. :D

item.title: Re: Huey, Stop Stealing This Author Commentary!
(no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read about the Hidebehind as a tyke ("The Desrick on Yandro", the first Manly Wade Wellman story I ever encountered). Curiously enough, didn't hear about Goatman until much later, and the Foundation introduced me to Sinning Jenny.

Coyote I've read about in Kage Baker's "Sky Coyote", a Company novel, and "Coyote Blues" by Christopher Moore.

This was a very satisfying look at what happens to beings of belief when the belief goes away. I hope that Coyote gets his back and doesn't become a Small God whispering in the wilderness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not being from the US, I had a little chuckle at the Hidebehind because the only reason it rang familiar to me was thanks to Dipper and his book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Threatening the Coyote with the Hidebehind was a stroke of genius and a terrific way to incorporate one of my favorite American legends into Sloth's Pit.

item.title: Huey, Stop Stealing This Author Commentary!
item.content:encoded:
(Only a few people will get the commentary joke this time. But meh!)

So, on SCP-2547's discussion page, Petrograd suggested that it visit Sloth Spit and see what would happen. After over a month of mulling and flip-flopping, I think it's ready to post.

Thanks to Petrograd for the idea, Niles for her blessing, and everyone who critiqued this on chat. Do what you will with it; upvote it, downvote it, read it and go "eh", hit it with magical tuning forks, whatever you want.
